revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=頼母子古墳(たのもしこふん) 別名:頼母子大塚山古墳(たのもしおおつかやまこふん)| 所在地=[[太田市]]牛沢町付近| 規模=不詳 高さ5.4m| 形状=不詳 (前方後円墳説が有力<ref>墳丘高さ5.4mから推測</ref>)| 築造年代=4世紀後半～末<ref>出土品から推測</ref>| 埋葬施設=不詳(竪穴式)| 出土品=[[銅鏡]]3面 石釧 鉄鏃30など| }}
==概要== 本古墳西側、かつて[[国道]]より南側に所在したと伝わる古墳。群馬県では[[大正4年]]の藤岡市三本木所在古墳での発見につぐ2番目に多い、2面の[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土した<ref>同じく[[高崎市]]柴崎蟹沢古墳、[[前橋市]]の[[前橋天神山古墳]]も2面</ref>。墳丘は[[明治43年]]に土砂採取のため破壊された。[[戸籍]]の記録や出土品から、[[朝子塚古墳]]の一つ前の首長墓とされている。埋葬施設は粘土郭とみられている。
==規模== 本古墳の規模について伝わる数値は墳丘高さのみである。
墳丘高さ 5.4[[m]]
==出土品== [[明治43年の]]削平時に波紋帯龍虎鏡、吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡、有銘文帯四神四獣鏡、石釧、鉄鏃などが発見された。 現在、吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡、有銘文帯四神四獣鏡は所在不明。
==吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡== 本古墳出土の吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡て推定されている拓本が[[奈良県]]立[[天理図書館]]に、拓影の鏡が[[南山大学]]人類学博物館にそれぞれ収蔵されている。現在この銅鏡は[[岐阜県]][[可児市]]土田出土とされ、兵庫県[[西求女塚古墳]]、京都府[[椿井大塚山古墳]]、千葉県[[城山1号墳]]出土の銅鏡と型を共有している可能性が高いことが指摘されている。
==脚注== 作成中
==参考文献== 作成中

墳丘高さ 5.4[[m]]
==出土品== [[明治43年]]の削平時に波紋帯龍虎鏡、吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡、有銘文帯四神四獣鏡、石釧、鉄鏃などが発見された。 現在、吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡、有銘文帯四神四獣鏡は所在不明。

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=頼母子古墳(たのもしこふん) 別名:頼母子大塚山古墳(たのもしおおつかやまこふん)| 所在地=[[太田市]]牛沢町付近| 規模=不詳 高さ5.4m| 形状=不詳 (前方後円墳説が有力<ref>墳丘高さ5.4mから推測</ref>)| 築造年代=4世紀後半～末<ref>出土品から推測</ref>| 埋葬施設=不詳(竪穴式)| 出土品=[[銅鏡]]3面 石釧 鉄鏃30など| }}
==概要== 本古墳西側、かつて[[国道]]より南側に所在したと伝わる古墳。群馬県では[[大正4年]]の藤岡市三本木所在古墳での発見につぐ2番目に多い、2面の[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土した<ref>同じく[[高崎市]]柴崎蟹沢古墳、[[前橋市]]の[[前橋天神山古墳]]も2面</ref>。墳丘は[[明治43年]]に土砂採取のため破壊された。[[戸籍]]の記録や出土品から、[[朝子塚古墳]]の一つ前の首長墓とされている。埋葬施設は粘土郭とみられている。
==規模== 本古墳の規模について伝わる数値は墳丘高さのみである。
墳丘高さ 5.4[[m]]
==出土品== [[明治43年]]の削平時に波紋帯龍虎鏡、吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡、有銘文帯四神四獣鏡、石釧、鉄鏃などが発見された。 現在、吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡、有銘文帯四神四獣鏡は所在不明。
==吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡== 本古墳出土の吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡と推定されている拓本が[[奈良県]]立[[天理図書館]]に、拓影の鏡が[[南山大学]]人類学博物館にそれぞれ収蔵されている。現在この銅鏡は[[岐阜県]][[可児市]]土田出土とされ、兵庫県[[西求女塚古墳]]、京都府[[椿井大塚山古墳]]、千葉県[[城山1号墳]]出土の銅鏡と同笵である。
==参考文献== 作成中
==脚注==

{{出典の明記|date=2020年2月}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 頼母子古墳 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=太田市}} |別名 = 頼母子大塚山古墳 |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[太田市]]牛沢町付近 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 15|緯度秒 = 32.0 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 04.5 |規模 = 不詳 高さ5.4 m |形状 = 不詳（前方後円墳説が有力<ref>墳丘高さ5.4m から推測</ref>） |築造年代 = 4世紀後半 - 末<ref>出土品から推測</ref> |埋葬施設 = 不詳（竪穴式） |出土品 = [[銅鏡]]3面 石釧 鉄鏃30など }} '''頼母子古墳'''（たのもしこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]]牛沢町付近に所在したと伝わる[[古墳]]。'''頼母子大塚山古墳'''（たのもしおおつかやまこふん）とも呼ばれる。
== 概要 == [[朝子塚古墳]]西側、かつて旧[[国道354号]]（[[群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線|群馬県道142号]]）より南側に所在したと伝わる古墳。群馬県では[[1915年]]（[[大正]]4年）の藤岡市三本木所在古墳での発見につぐ2番目に多い、2面の[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土した<ref>同じく[[高崎市]]柴崎蟹沢古墳、[[前橋市]]の[[前橋天神山古墳]]も2面</ref>。墳丘は[[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）に土砂採取のため破壊された。[[戸籍]]の記録や出土品から、[[朝子塚古墳]]の一つ前の首長墓とされている。埋葬施設は粘土郭とみられている。
== 規模 == 本古墳の規模について伝わる数値は墳丘高さのみである。
* 墳丘高さ : 5.4 [[メートル|m]]
== 出土品 == 1910年の削平時に波紋帯龍虎鏡、吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡、有銘文帯四神四獣鏡、石釧、鉄鏃などが発見された。 現在、吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡、有銘文帯四神四獣鏡は所在不明。
== 吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡 == 本古墳出土の吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡と推定されている拓本が{{要出典範囲|奈良県立天理図書館|date=2020年2月}}に、拓影の鏡が[[南山大学]]人類学博物館にそれぞれ収蔵されている。現在この銅鏡は[[岐阜県]][[可児市]]土田出土とされ、兵庫県[[西求女塚古墳]]、京都府[[椿井大塚山古墳]]、千葉県[[城山1号墳]]出土の銅鏡と同笵である。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == {{節スタブ}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 銅鏃（群馬県太田市牛沢字頼母子大塚山古墳出土）] - 東京国立博物館 研究情報アーカイブズ

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020年2月}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 頼母子古墳 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=太田市}} |別名 = 頼母子大塚山古墳 |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[太田市]]牛沢町付近 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 15|緯度秒 = 32.0 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 04.5 |規模 = 不詳 高さ5.4 m |形状 = 不詳（前方後円墳説が有力<ref>墳丘高さ5.4m から推測</ref>） |築造年代 = 4世紀後半 - 末<ref>出土品から推測</ref> |埋葬施設 = 不詳（竪穴式） |出土品 = [[銅鏡]]3面 石釧 鉄鏃30など }} '''頼母子古墳'''（たのもしこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]]牛沢町付近に所在したと伝わる[[古墳]]。'''頼母子大塚山古墳'''（たのもしおおつかやまこふん）とも呼ばれる。
== 概要 == [[朝子塚古墳]]西側、かつて旧[[国道354号]]（[[群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線|群馬県道142号]]）より南側に所在したと伝わる古墳。群馬県では[[1915年]]（[[大正]]4年）の藤岡市三本木所在古墳での発見につぐ2番目に多い、2面の[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土した<ref>同じく[[高崎市]]柴崎蟹沢古墳、[[前橋市]]の[[前橋天神山古墳]]も2面</ref>。墳丘は[[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）に土砂採取のため破壊された。[[戸籍]]の記録や出土品から、[[朝子塚古墳]]の一つ前の首長墓とされている。埋葬施設は粘土郭とみられている。
== 規模 == 本古墳の規模について伝わる数値は墳丘高さのみである。
* 墳丘高さ : 5.4 [[メートル|m]]
== 出土品 == 1910年の削平時に波紋帯龍虎鏡、吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡、有銘文帯四神四獣鏡、石釧、鉄鏃などが発見された。 現在、吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡、有銘文帯四神四獣鏡は所在不明。
== 吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡 == 本古墳出土の吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡と推定されている拓本が{{要出典範囲|奈良県立天理図書館|date=2020年2月}}に、拓影の鏡が[[南山大学]]人類学博物館にそれぞれ収蔵されている。現在この銅鏡は[[岐阜県]][[可児市]]土田出土とされ、兵庫県[[西求女塚古墳]]、京都府[[椿井大塚山古墳]]、千葉県[[城山1号墳 (香取市)|城山1号墳]]出土の銅鏡と同笵である。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == {{節スタブ}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 銅鏃（群馬県太田市牛沢字頼母子大塚山古墳出土）] - 東京国立博物館 研究情報アーカイブズ

{{More citations needed|date=February 2020}} {{Japanese burial mound |name = Tanomoshi Kofun |image = {{Image requested|cat=Ōta, Gunma}} |alias = Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, near Ushizawa Town |latitude_deg = 36|latitude_min = 15|latitude_sec = 32.0 |longitude_deg = 139|longitude_min = 21|longitude_sec = 04.5 |size = Unknown, height 5.4 m |shape = Unknown (hypothesized to be a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}<ref>Estimated based on the mound height of 5.4m</ref>) |construction_period = Late 4th century<ref>Estimated based on excavated artifacts</ref> |burial_chamber = Unknown (likely pit-style) |artifacts = Three {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, stone bracelets, 30 iron arrowheads, etc. }}
'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== Dimensions ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== Excavated Artifacts ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{Stub section}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 Iron arrowheads excavated from Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun, Ōta City, Gunma Prefecture] – Tokyo National Museum Research Archives

'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== Dimensions ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== Excavated Artifacts ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{Stub section}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 Iron arrowheads excavated from Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun, Ōta City, Gunma Prefecture] – Tokyo National Museum Research Archives
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== Dimensions ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== Excavated Artifacts ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)

'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== Dimensions ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== Excavated Artifacts ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{Stub section}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 Iron arrowheads excavated from Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun, Ōta City, Gunma Prefecture] – Tokyo National Museum Research Archives

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tamonoko Burial Mound-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Tamonoko Burial Mound]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Tamonoko Burial Mound]][[ja:%E9%A0%BC%E6%AF%8D%E5%AD%90%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tamonoko Burial Mound]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tamonoko Burial Mound]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{More citations needed|date=February 2020}} {{Japanese burial mound |name = Tanomoshi Kofun |image = {{image requested|cat=Ōta, Gunma}} |alias = Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, near Ushizawa Town |latitude_deg = 36|latitude_min = 15|latitude_sec = 32.0 |longitude_deg = 139|longitude_min = 21|longitude_sec = 04.5 |size = Unknown, height 5.4 m |type = Unknown (hypothesized to be a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref>Estimated based on the mound height of 5.4m</ref>) |construction_period = Late 4th century<ref>Estimated based on excavated artifacts</ref> |burial_chamber = Unknown (likely pit-style) |artifacts = Three {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, stone bracelets, 30 iron arrowheads, etc. }}
'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== Dimensions ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== Excavated Artifacts ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{Stub section}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 Iron arrowheads excavated from Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun, Ōta City, Gunma Prefecture] – Tokyo National Museum Research Archives

A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)

{{More citations needed|date=February 2020}} {{Japanese burial mound |name = Tanomoshi Kofun |image = {{image requested|cat=Ōta, Gunma}} |alias = Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, near Ushizawa Town |latitude_deg = 36|latitude_min = 15|latitude_sec = 32.0 |longitude_deg = 139|longitude_min = 21|longitude_sec = 04.5 |size = Unknown, height 5.4 m |type = Unknown (hypothesized to be a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref>Estimated based on the mound height of 5.4m</ref>) |construction_period = Late 4th century<ref>Estimated based on excavated artifacts</ref> |burial_chamber = Unknown (likely pit-style) |artifacts = Three {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, stone bracelets, 30 iron arrowheads, etc. }}
'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== Dimensions ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== Excavated Artifacts ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)

It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== Dimensions ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== Excavated Artifacts ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)

[[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamonoko Burial Mound]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Tamonoko Burial Mound-->[[category:qq close]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tamonoko Burial Mound]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Tamonoko Burial Mound]][[ja:%E9%A0%BC%E6%AF%8D%E5%AD%90%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tamonoko Burial Mound]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tamonoko Burial Mound]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Tamonoko Burial Mound]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamonoko Burial Mound]][[Category:qq]]

{{More citations needed|date=February 2020}} {{Japanese burial mound |name = Tanomoshi Kofun |image = {{image requested|cat=Ōta, Gunma}} |alias = Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, near Ushizawa Town |latitude_deg = 36|latitude_min = 15|latitude_sec = 32.0 |longitude_deg = 139|longitude_min = 21|longitude_sec = 04.5 |size = Unknown, height 5.4 m |type = Unknown (hypothesized to be a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref>Estimated based on the mound height of 5.4m</ref>) |construction_period = Late 4th century<ref>Estimated based on excavated artifacts</ref> |burial_chamber = Unknown (likely pit-style) |artifacts = Three {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, stone bracelets, 30 iron arrowheads, etc. }}
'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== Dimensions ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== Excavated Artifacts ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{Stub section}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 Iron arrowheads excavated from Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun, Ōta City, Gunma Prefecture] – Tokyo National Museum Research Archives

{{More citations needed|date=February 2020}} {{Japanese burial mound |name = Tanomoshi Kofun |image = {{image requested|cat=Ōta, Gunma}} |alias = Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, near Ushizawa Town |latitude_deg = 36|latitude_min = 15|latitude_sec = 32.0 |longitude_deg = 139|longitude_min = 21|longitude_sec = 04.5 |size = Unknown, height 5.4 m |type = Unknown (hypothesized to be a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref>Estimated based on the mound height of 5.4m</ref>) |construction_period = Late 4th century<ref>Estimated based on excavated artifacts</ref> |burial_chamber = Unknown (likely pit-style) |artifacts = Three {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, stone bracelets, 30 iron arrowheads, etc. }}
'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== Dimensions ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== Excavated Artifacts ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{Stub section}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 Iron arrowheads excavated from Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun, Ōta City, Gunma Prefecture] – Tokyo National Museum Research Archives
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== Dimensions ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== Excavated Artifacts ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)

This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== Dimensions ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== Excavated Artifacts ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{Stub section}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 Iron arrowheads excavated from Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun, Ōta City, Gunma Prefecture] – Tokyo National Museum Research Archives

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{More citations needed|date=February 2020}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Tanomoshi Kofun |image = {{image requested|cat=Ōta, Gunma}} |alias = Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, near Ushizawa Town |latitude_deg = 36|latitude_min = 15|latitude_sec = 32.0 |longitude_deg = 139|longitude_min = 21|longitude_sec = 04.5 |size = Unknown, height 5.4 m |type = Unknown (hypothesized to be a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref>Estimated based on the mound height of 5.4m</ref>) |construction_period = Late 4th century<ref>Estimated based on excavated artifacts</ref> |burial_chamber = Unknown (likely pit-style) |artifacts = Three {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, stone bracelets, 30 iron arrowheads, etc. }}
'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== Dimensions ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== Excavated Artifacts ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{Stub section}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 Iron arrowheads excavated from Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun, Ōta City, Gunma Prefecture] – Tokyo National Museum Research Archives
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{More citations needed|date=February 2020}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Tanomoshi Kofun |image = {{image requested|cat=Ōta, Gunma}} |alias = Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, near Ushizawa Town |latitude_deg = 36|latitude_min = 15|latitude_sec = 32.0 |longitude_deg = 139|longitude_min = 21|longitude_sec = 04.5 |size = Unknown, height 5.4 m |type = Unknown (hypothesized to be a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref>Estimated based on the mound height of 5.4m</ref>) |construction_period = Late 4th century<ref>Estimated based on excavated artifacts</ref> |burial_chamber = Unknown (likely pit-style) |artifacts = Three {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, stone bracelets, 30 iron arrowheads, etc. }}
'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== Dimensions ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== Excavated Artifacts ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{Stub section}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 Iron arrowheads excavated from Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun, Ōta City, Gunma Prefecture] – Tokyo National Museum Research Archives

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{More citations needed|date=February 2020}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Tanomoshi Kofun |image = {{image requested|cat=Ōta, Gunma}} |alias = Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, near Ushizawa Town |latitude_deg = 36|latitude_min = 15|latitude_sec = 32.0 |longitude_deg = 139|longitude_min = 21|longitude_sec = 04.5 |size = Unknown, height 5.4 m |type = Unknown (hypothesized to be a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref>Estimated based on the mound height of 5.4m</ref>) |construction_period = Late 4th century<ref>Estimated based on excavated artifacts</ref> |burial_chamber = Unknown (likely pit-style) |burial goods = Three {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, stone bracelets, 30 iron arrowheads, etc. }}
'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== Dimensions ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== Excavated Artifacts ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{Stub section}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 Iron arrowheads excavated from Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun, Ōta City, Gunma Prefecture] – Tokyo National Museum Research Archives
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{More citations needed|date=February 2020}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Tanomoshi Kofun |image = {{image requested|cat=Ōta, Gunma}} |alternate_name = Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, near Ushizawa Town |latitude_deg = 36|latitude_min = 15|latitude_sec = 32.0 |longitude_deg = 139|longitude_min = 21|longitude_sec = 04.5 |size = Unknown, height 5.4 m |type = Unknown (hypothesized to be a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref>Estimated based on the mound height of 5.4m</ref>) |built = Late 4th century<ref>Estimated based on excavated artifacts</ref> |burial_chamber = Unknown (likely pit-style) |burial goods = Three {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, stone bracelets, 30 iron arrowheads, etc. }}
'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== area ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== burial goods ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: - **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** - **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** - **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{Stub section}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 Iron arrowheads excavated from Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun, Ōta City, Gunma Prefecture] – Tokyo National Museum Research Archives

The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)

'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== area ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== burial goods ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: ** **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** ** **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** ** **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)

'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the former {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the artifacts found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== area ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== burial goods ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following artifacts were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: ** **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** ** **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** ** **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{More citations needed|date=February 2020}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Tanomoshi Kofun |image = {{image requested|cat=Ōta, Gunma}} |alternate_name = Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, near Ushizawa Town |coordinates = 36|coordinates = 15|coordinates = 32.0 |coordinates = 139|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 04.5 |size = Unknown, height 5.4 m |type = Unknown (hypothesized to be a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref>Estimated based on the mound height of 5.4m</ref>) |built = Late 4th century<ref>Estimated based on excavated burial goods</ref> |burial_chamber = Unknown (likely pit-style) |burial goods = Three {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, stone bracelets, 30 iron arrowheads, etc. }}
'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the typeer {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the burial goods found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== area ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== burial goods ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following burial goods were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: ** **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** ** **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** ** **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{Stub section}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 Iron arrowheads excavated from Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun, Ōta City, Gunma Prefecture] – Tokyo National Museum Research Archives

'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the typeer {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the burial goods found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== area ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== burial goods ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following burial goods were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: ** **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** ** **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** ** **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{More citations needed|date=February 2020}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Tanomoshi Kofun |image = {{image requested|cat=Ōta, Gunma}} |alternate_name = Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, near Ushizawa Town |coordinates = 36|coordinates = 15|coordinates = 32.0 |coordinates = 139|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 04.5 |size = Unknown, height 5.4 m |type = Unknown (hypothesized to be a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref>Estimated based on the mound height of 5.4m</ref>) |built = Late 4th century<ref>Estimated based on excavated burial goods</ref> |burial = Unknown (likely pit-style) |burial goods = Three {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, stone bracelets, 30 iron arrowheads, etc. }}
'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the typeer {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the burial goods found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== area ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== burial goods ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following burial goods were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: ** **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** ** **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** ** **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{Stub section}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 Iron arrowheads excavated from Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun, Ōta City, Gunma Prefecture] – Tokyo National Museum Research Archives

The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)

'''Tanomoshi Kofun''' (頼母子古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that was once located near Ushizawa Town, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is also known as '''Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (頼母子大塚山古墳).
== Overview ==
This kofun was once located west of the {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}}, south of the typeer {{ill|National Route 354 (Japan)|ja|国道354号}} (now {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 142|ja|群馬県道142号綿貫篠塚線}}). It is notable for the discovery of two {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} (triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirrors), the second-highest number found in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, following a discovery in 1915 at a kofun in {{ill|Mihongi, Fujioka|ja|藤岡市三本木}}.<ref>Similar discoveries were made at {{ill|Shibasaki Kanisawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki, Gunma|ja|高崎市}} and {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|1=Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}.</ref>
The mound was destroyed in **1910 (Meiji 43)** for soil extraction. Based on census records and the burial goods found, it is considered **the predecessor to the Asakozuka Kofun’s chief burial**. The burial chamber is believed to have been a **clay coffin-style (nendokaku)**.
== area ==
The only surviving measurement of the kofun is its mound height:
* Mound height: **5.4** [[meter|m]]
== burial goods ==
During the 1910 excavation, the following burial goods were discovered: - **Three bronze mirrors**: ** **Ripple-pattern dragon and tiger mirror (波紋帯龍虎鏡)** ** **Gosaku-inscribed belt mirror with three deities and five beasts (吾作銘帯三神五獣鏡)** ** **Inscribed belt mirror with four deities and four beasts (有銘文帯四神四獣鏡)** - **Stone bracelets** - **Iron arrowheads** (approximately 30)
The **whereabouts of the Gosaku-inscribed mirror and the inscribed four-deity mirror are currently unknown**.
== Gosaku-Inscribed Belt Mirror with Three Deities and Five Beasts ==
A rubbing of this mirror, presumed to be the one from this kofun, is stored at the **Nara Prefectural Tenri Library**{{Citation needed|date=February 2020}}, and a replica is preserved at the **Anthropology Museum of Nanzan University**.
The mirror is now attributed to an excavation in **Tsuchida, {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市}}, Gifu Prefecture**, and is **from the same mold as mirrors discovered at**: - **{{ill|Saikumezuka Kofun|ja|西求女塚古墳}}** (Hyōgo Prefecture) - **{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}** (Kyoto Prefecture) - **{{ill|Shiroyama No.1 Kofun (Katori, Chiba)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}}** (Chiba Prefecture)

== External Links == * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0042738 Iron arrowheads excavated from Tanomoshi Ōtsukayama Kofun, Ōta City, Gunma Prefecture] – Tokyo National Museum Research Archives
